campbell.title: Campbell City Council — March 24, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council held a meeting on March 24, 2026, where they honored K9 Lucas, a police department dog who recently passed away. The council also moved forward with updates to the city's beekeeping rules, discussed a state bill about development near transit areas (with some council members stepping aside due to conflicts of interest), and reviewed a study about potentially creating homeless services in the West Valley region.
keyTopics: K9 Lucas memorial – police department's first working dog
Updated beekeeping ordinance approved
Senate Bill 79 hearing on transit-oriented development
West Valley regional homeless services feasibility study
FY 2025 financial report accepted

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 31, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a special meeting on March 31, 2026, to review and approve the yearly work plans for five city commissions that handle everything from parks and libraries to traffic safety and historic preservation. Each commission presented their priorities and goals for the next fiscal year, and the council also discussed a legal matter in a closed session.
keyTopics: Approval of work plans for Heritage Preservation, Library & Public Art, Parks & Recreation, Traffic Safety, and Planning Commissions
FY 2026-27 commission priorities and goals
Litigation matter (details discussed in closed session)

los-altos.title: Los Altos City Council — March 24, 2026
los-altos.summary: The Los Altos City Council held their March 24, 2026 meeting to approve routine administrative items like meeting minutes and financial reports, while also establishing new council procedures and rules. The council approved raising the Progress Pride and Juneteenth flags, heard a presentation from the transit authority (VTA), and discussed plans for implementing the city's housing goals and addressing weeds on public land.
keyTopics: New Council Norms and Procedures adopted
Below Market Rate (BMR) affordable housing unit transfer rules updated
Weed abatement completion report finalized
Progress Pride and Juneteenth flag raising approvals
VTA transit presentation and housing element implementation schedule

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — March 17, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on March 17, 2026, to approve meeting minutes, review the city's finances from January, and check on progress toward housing and general development goals. The council also approved a new residential development project (Solana) and finalized plans for community health and senior services for the year ahead.
keyTopics: Approval of Solana Project development (Tract 10657)
January 2026 financial and investment report
Progress update on housing and general planning goals
2026 community health and senior services programs
Changes to how council meetings are recorded and formatted

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 1, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting on April 1, 2026 to prepare for upcoming council sessions on April 7 and April 14. The council reviewed what items would be on the agendas for those meetings, decided whether any items needed specific time slots, and checked if any documents needed special notice requirements. They also reviewed public records from the previous week.
keyTopics: Finalized the April 7, 2026 council meeting agenda
Prepared draft agenda for April 14, 2026 meeting
Set time limits for specific agenda items where needed
Reviewed public records from March 19-26, 2026

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — March 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: This was a routine Mountain View City Council meeting on March 30, 2026, focused on standard administrative items like approving previous meeting minutes and allowing residents to speak during public comment. The council also reviewed reports from various city committees and boards, with information about upcoming meetings posted in advance.
keyTopics: Public comment period for residents to voice concerns
Approval of previous meeting minutes
City committee and board meeting schedules
Documents available at City Clerk's Office (500 Castro Street)
Questions directed to Recreation Division (650-903-6331)

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — March 25, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met to review applications for federal grant money that helps fund affordable housing and community development projects in Sunnyvale. The meeting included approval of minutes from the previous month and presentations from organizations seeking funding to help local residents.
keyTopics: Federal grant applications for housing and community programs
Review of affordable housing and human services funding
Approval of February meeting minutes
Community organizations pitching for development block grants

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — March 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council focused on helping local businesses grow and thrive. They discussed ways to reach out to shops at local shopping centers, get more businesses involved in city programs, and offer tax breaks or other incentives to encourage new retail stores and small companies to open in Cupertino.
keyTopics: Business License Amnesty Program to help shops get compliant with city rules
Summer Shop Local campaign to encourage residents to buy from Cupertino businesses
Partnerships with private companies to sponsor city events and festivals
Special incentives (tax breaks, grants, etc.) to attract new retail and startup companies

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 2, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara's Historical and Landmarks Commission is holding a hearing on April 2, 2026, where residents can attend in person or join online via Zoom. The meeting will review and discuss historical properties and landmarks in the city. If you want to weigh in, you can submit written comments by email before noon on the meeting day.
keyTopics: Historical and Landmarks Commission hearing
Hybrid meeting format (in-person + Zoom option)
Public comment submission deadline: noon on April 2

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — March 17, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held a routine meeting to handle administrative tasks like approving previous meeting notes and planning upcoming agendas. The council also approved a trip for Mayor Montano to attend the WELL 2026 conference and confirmed a new representative from Milpitas Unified School District to serve on the city's economic committee.
keyTopics: Approved previous City Council meeting minutes
Mayor Montano authorized to attend WELL 2026 Annual Conference
New School District representative confirmed for Economic Development committee
Reviewed requests from councilmembers for future agenda items

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met on March 23, 2026 to recognize Women's History Month and approve two important agreements. The council authorized the city to partner with the American Red Cross to use city facilities during emergencies, and approved spending about $93,400 per year for three years on email security and data protection services (Mimecast software).
keyTopics: Women's History Month proclamation
Emergency disaster response facility agreement with American Red Cross
Three-year email security software contract ($280,000+ total cost)
